text.title: Ruby ActiveRecord Patterns Examples
text.summary: Worked example: harden Order model with scopes, enum, eager-loading, and transactional create.

example.context: Rails 7 commerce app; Order model with free-string status; OrdersController#index does Order.all.each { |o| o.customer.name }; OrderService#create does Order.create + InventoryItem.update outside tx.
example.walk: Step 1: scope :recent = -> { where('created_at >= ?', 30.days.ago) }; scope :paid = -> { where.not(paid_at: nil) }. Step 2: Order.includes(:customer, :items).recent at index. Step 3: migration changes status to integer; enum status: {pending:0, paid:1, shipped:2, cancelled:3}; queries become Order.pending. Step 4: OrderService#create wraps both in ActiveRecord::Base.transaction. Step 5: rubocop-rails Rails/TimeZone enabled; 12 Time.now replaced with Time.current.
example.output: Index 51 → 3 queries; status typos impossible; multi-write atomic; timezone consistent.
